assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check for deprecated APIs and specifically mentions 1.32 removed several beta APIs with kubectl get events command to find deprecated API usage

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check validatingwebhookconfigurations and mutatingwebhookconfigurations under 'Quick Recovery Actions'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check API server resource limits with kubectl describe pod and mentions API server may need more resources after upgrade

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check API server pods, etcd pods, and look for pod restart patterns with specific kubectl commands

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes the exact command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_duration_seconds' in the monitoring section

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes specific section on 'Network Policy Changes' with commands to check networkpolicies across all namespaces

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check events with kubectl get events and mentions checking for ImagePull issues and pod restart patterns

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) specifically or suggest checking their behavior after the upgrade
